sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Betty, a 48-year-old female, reported being thankful to be alive after having a heart attack, which resulted in the placement of a stent in her right coronary artery.
She has been experiencing some struggles and anxiety since the incident.
She has been taking it easy, doing light work, and following her medication regimen.
She reported feeling a little short of breath when walking up and downstairs.
She has had no chest pain since the stent was placed.
She has been taking her medications, including brilinta and baby aspirin, as prescribed.
She has had some soreness in her wrist from the cardiac catheterization but it is healing.
She is a Denver Nuggets fan and mentioned that they are still in the NBA playoffs.
She has been eating well and trying to watch her salt intake.
Past medical history: The patient had a heart attack with a stent placement in her right coronary artery.
She had a 48-hour hospital stay.
Her ejection fraction, a measure of the heart's pumping function, was 50%, which is considered low normal.
Review of systems: The patient reported some shortness of breath when walking up and downstairs.
Current medications:
Brilinta 90mg twice daily
Aspirin (baby aspirin) once daily
Crestor 40mg once daily
Toprol 50mg once daily
Objective:
Vital signs:
Blood pressure: 127/60
Neck exam: No jugular venous distention, no carotid bruits
Heart exam: Slight 3/6 systolic ejection murmur at the left base
Lung exam: Lungs are clear to auscultation bilaterally
Musculoskeletal exam: Right radial artery cardiac cath site is clean, dry, and intact with a palpable radial pulse
Assessment / Problem List:

Assessment: The patient is a 48-year-old female with a history of inferior myocardial infarction, currently on a medication regimen, and experiencing some shortness of breath when walking up and downstairs.
Problem list: Myocardial infarction; Mitral regurgitation; Hyperlipidemia; Hypertension
Plan:
I prescribed Brilinta 90mg twice daily for at least one year to prevent stent thrombosis.
I prescribed Crestor 40mg once daily to manage her lipid levels.
I prescribed Toprol 50mg once daily to manage her hypertension.
I ordered a repeat echocardiogram in six months to assess for any changes in her mitral regurgitation.
I also ordered liver function studies and a lipid panel to follow up with the next visit.
I advised her to continue taking aspirin lifelong.
I recommended cardiac rehab to provide dietary education and counseling, and to help her regain confidence in exercising.
Follow-up:
I want to see the patient again in six weeks to follow up on her echocardiogram, liver function studies, and lipid panel results.
